data.title: Dow jumps 800 points on rate-cut optimism; hitting all-time high; Nvidia earnings on horizon.
data.description: On Friday, the Dow Jones index reached an all-time high, surging by 800 points, driven by growing optimism regarding a potential rate cut at the September Federal Reserve meeting. This rise was fueled by expectations shaped by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's comments. Additionally, investor attention is focused on Nvidia's forthcoming earnings report. The overall sentiment in the market is positive, with indicators pointing towards a favorable outlook fuelled by these developments.

data.title: Derek Bryson Park's post-9/11 financial recovery role highlighted in Enterprise Chronicles article.
data.description: A prominent business publication, Enterprise Chronicles, highlighted Derek Bryson Park in a detailed article titled “The Untold Story of Derek Bryson Park and Financial Rescue after the 9/11 Attacks”. The article delves into Mr. Park's significant yet overlooked contributions to stabilizing the finances post the September 11, 2001, tragedies. It sheds light on his pivotal role in the financial recovery efforts following the devastating events, emphasizing his critical involvement in ensuring financial stability during that tumultuous period.

data.title: Predicting HBAR reaching $0.26-$0.35 in 2-4 weeks, technical indicators suggest bullish reversal.
data.description: The price prediction for HBAR indicates a potential resistance test at $0.26 and a potential rally to $0.35 by late September. Technical indicators suggest oversold conditions, signaling a possible reversal in the near future.

data.title: Trump's economic approval ratings are resembling Biden's, similar to his predecessor; numbers are on track.
data.description: The current economic approval ratings of President Donald Trump are showing a resemblance to the ratings received by his predecessor.

data.title: Safe Bulkers, Inc. sells 2006-built Kamsarmax vessel MV Pedhoulas Merchant for $11.5 million in September 2025.
data.description: In Monaco on August 12, 2025, Safe Bulkers, Inc., a global marine drybulk transportation services provider, revealed the sale agreement for the MV Pedhoulas Merchant. The Japanese-built vessel, falling under the Kamsarmax class and constructed in 2006, is set to be sold for $11.5 million. The delivery of the ship to the new owner is scheduled for September 2025.

data.title: Ethereum: Exchange ‘flux’ goes negative for the first time, hinting at Ether’s upward potential.
data.description: For the first time ever, Ether's exchange flux metric has gone negative, indicating a change in investor patterns and suggesting that Ether may be poised to continue its upward trend. This metric is a significant development in the cryptocurrency world.

data.title: Despite hitting record highs midweek, U.S. equities faltered Friday due to inflation concerns. Core PCE index rose 2.9% YoY in July, highest in 5 months. US goods trade deficit surged to $103.6 billion in July. Fed's rate decisions now uncertain.
data.description: U.S. equities reached record highs during the week but saw a decline by Friday due to inflation indicators impacting the Federal Reserve's interest rate decisions. The GLD ETF is also trading close to its record peak. The Core Personal Consumption Expenditures price index, a key inflation measure for the Federal Reserve, increased by 2.9% year-over-year in July, the highest in five months. Despite recent hints from Fed Chair Jerome Powell about potential rate cuts, the latest inflation data has introduced uncertainty, notably as inflation exceeded the Fed's 2% target. Additionally, the U.S. goods trade deficit rose significantly to $103.6 billion in July, a substantial increase from June. This information reveals potential economic challenges ahead. Check live prices for further updates.

data.description: The arrest of numerous Koreans at the location garnered a surprised yet restrained response, highlighting the precarious position of a government in the midst of intense trade discussions with the Trump administration.

data.title: LDO price forecast: Aiming for $1.63 breakout, predicting 40% surge to $2.10 by October 2025 despite bearish signals.
data.description: According to forecasts, the potential breakout of the LDO price above the $1.63 resistance level could potentially lead Lido DAO to reach $2.10 in the next 4 to 6 weeks. This positive trajectory is anticipated even with the current bearish signals in the market.

data.title: Chijet Motor Co. Inc. issued 640,850 class A ordinary shares to CVR holders on record as of July 22, 2025.
data.description: Chijet Motor Company, Inc. (NASDAQ: CJET) disclosed today that they have distributed 640,850 class A ordinary shares valued at US$0.003 per share to holders of contingent value rights (CVRs) as of July 22, 2025. These shares were issued to Qualifying CVR Holders on a pro rata basis according to the number of CVRs they possessed. The record date for eligibility to receive the shares was also on July 22, 2025. Chijet Motor Company is a prominent high-tech firm specializing in the production, distribution, and maintenance of both traditional fuel and new energy vehicles.

data.title: Nasdaq proposes new listing standards with enhanced requirements for public float and IPO capital raised, and stricter delisting procedures for non-compliant companies.
data.description: In New York on September 3, 2025, Nasdaq announced revisions to its listing standards to support capital formation, protect investors, and maintain market integrity. The proposed changes include stricter criteria for minimum company public float and capital raised in IPOs. Additionally, Nasdaq suggested more rigorous procedures for suspending and delisting companies that do not meet continued listing standards. These updates aim to strengthen Nasdaq's commitment to fostering a robust marketplace.

data.title: Dow Jones rises, S&P breaks record, Nvidia earnings in focus. Kohl's stock soars. Live coverage on Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: On Wednesday, the Dow Jones index experienced a surge in anticipation of Nvidia's earnings release, while Kohl's stock witnessed a significant rise. The stock market saw the Dow rallying towards the end of the trading session as the S&P broke a new record. Investors were closely monitoring Nvidia's earnings report.

data.title: Regulators hint at 24/7 capital markets, which could bring new opportunities and risks to traditional markets not open round the clock.
data.description: The implementation of a round-the-clock trading cycle would introduce both advantages and vulnerabilities to conventional financial markets that currently do not operate outside of regular business hours.

data.title: Trump Administration considering declaring national housing emergency, citing affordability concerns. XLRE ETF up 4% YTD. Treasury Secretary Bessent hints at cost-cutting and supply expansion measures.
data.description: The Trump administration is reportedly discussing the possibility of declaring a national housing emergency this fall, a measure not used since the 2008 financial crisis. This decision comes as concerns over affordability continue to rise and Republicans gear up for the 2026 midterms. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent shared during a Labor Day breakfast interview in McLean, Virginia that affordability will be a major focus for the administration moving forward. The XLRE ETF has seen a 4% increase year to date. Bessent mentioned potential steps to reduce costs and increase housing supply as part of the administration's strategy. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.
